title: Retinal Microstructure Imaging Using OCT
author: David A. Antonetti
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yabfsan

steps.title: The eyes of conscious animals are...
steps.instruction: The eyes of conscious animals are dilated by first applying a small drops (~10 µl) of tropicamide (1%) followed by a small drop of phenylephrine (2.5%) 2 to 3 minutes later

steps.title: Animal is then sedated with ketamine and xylazine
steps.instruction: Animal is then sedated with ketamine and xylazine

steps.title: After sedation, the corneas are kept...
steps.instruction: After sedation, the corneas are kept moist with the wetting agents (Systane)

steps.title: Animal is then placed on a...
steps.instruction: Animal is then placed on a platform enabling three-dimensional fine movements

steps.title: The camera lens is adjusted to...
steps.instruction: The camera lens is adjusted to aim at the eye to acquire images without physical contact

steps.title: Volume or radial scan (1
steps.instruction: Volume or radial scan (1.4 x 1.4 mm) is obtained from each eye with a Bioptigen Envisu R2200 preclinical spectral domain optical coherence tomography (SD-OCT) system

steps.title: After imaging procedure, the animal is...
steps.instruction: After imaging procedure, the animal is allowed to recover from the anesthesia

steps.title: Bioptigen Diver software is used to...
steps.instruction: Bioptigen Diver software is used to analyze retinal structure from the image obtained
